records.ety: [OE. shriken, originallythe same word as E. screech. See Screech, and cf. Screak.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shrieked (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shrieking.]

defs: To utter a loud, sharp, shrill sound or cry, as do some birds and beasts; to scream, as in a sudden fright, in horror or anguish.
quotes: It was the owl that shrieked.
At this she shrieked aloud; the mournful train Echoed her grief.

defs: To utter sharply and shrilly; to utter in or with a shriek or shrieks.
quotes: On top whereof aye dwelt the ghostly owl, Shrieking his baleful note.
She shrieked his name To the dark woods.

defs: A sharp, shrill outcry or scream; a shrill wild cry such as is caused by sudden or extreme terror, pain, or the like.
quotes: Shrieks, clamors, murmurs, fill the frighted town.
